user_message: Can you extract the migration architecture from this meeting transcript for Adatum Corporation? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-01-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:10:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:18:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-O trap (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-O failure mode (Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-O -- Post-meeting flow has no native learning loop from prior follow-ups
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
